title: Publications
text:
Here are some of my publications:
Hawkins, E., Brohan, P., Burgess, S.N., Burt, S., Compo, G.P., Gray, S.L., Haigh, I.D., Hersbach, H., Kuijjer, K., Martínez-Alvarado, O. and McColl, C., (2023). Rescuing historical weather observations improves quantification of severe windstorm risks. Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences 23(4), 1465-1482. https://doi.org/10.5194/nhess-23-1465-2023.
Kuijjer, E.K., Haigh, I.D., Marsh, R., Farr, H., (2022). Changing tidal dynamics and the role of the marine environment in the maritime migration to Australasia. PaleoAnthropology 2022(1).https://doi.org/10.48738/2022.iss1.105
Kuijjer, E.K., (2020) The role of open ocean and coastal tidal currents in the maritime migration to Sahul, ca. 65,000–50,000 years ago. University of Southampton, Doctoral Thesis, 329pp. http://eprints.soton.ac.uk/id/eprint/446902
Kuijjer, E. K., Mbye, H., Power, C., Veluplay, G., Westbury, S., (2017). Social Sustainability and Unplanned Urban Growth in the Coastal Megacity. Southampton, UK: University of Southampton.

title: Publications
text:
Podcast appearances
I am featured on Before Us podcast by Helen Farr and Erich Fisher, talking about early seafaring, ocean currents and winds. Check out Season 1 Episode 12: Going with the flow.

title: Home
text:
I’m Kiki Kuijjer, I help people and organisations connect audiences with research, collections, and cultural heritage through digital tools and communication.
I work in Digital Scholarship at the University of Southampton, focusing on 2D digitisation and helping people connect with library collections. I also manage digital engagement for the Ocean Decade Heritage Network, supporting efforts to better integrate ocean heritage into the UN Ocean Decade.
As a freelancer, I offer consultancy, social media support, website management, and science communication services.
About me

title: Conference hashtags on Bluesky
text:
Inspired by Alice Cann (Librarian AND/OR researcher), I plan to list conference hashtags related to Ocean Heritage on this page.
Check out Alice’s Library conferences on BlueSky.

title: Welcome to my blog
text:
I’m starting this blog to share ideas and projects at the crossroads of digital scholarship and ocean heritage.
By day, I work in Digital Scholarship at the University of Southampton helping make library collections more open, accessible, and engaging. I also support ocean advocacy through digital work with the Ocean Decade Heritage Network.
This blog is a place for me to think out loud. Expect posts research communication, digital tools, maybe some code, and probably a few ocean-related tangents too.
All views here are my own.
